dict.string:
Orion Status — a daily Artemis mission log. The ISS-tracker idea, pointed at the Moon. Run it once a day, or set Automation: Shortcuts ▸ Automation ▸ Time of Day ▸ Daily.
WHAT IT DOES (all accessible to Shortcuts): • Fetches live Orion capsule telemetry from your own worker (/position): distance from Earth + Moon, velocity, mission phase, crew, and which Deep Space Network dish is talking to it. • Fetches the latest mission schedule + research notes (Starship propellant transfer is the linchpin gate before any lunar landing). • An on-device AAPL Foundation Model reasons over BOTH sources and writes a one-screen "today's mission update" log. • Appends that log entry to a running Orion Log note, then notifies you.
HONEST BOUNDARY: Orion is NASA/Lockheed's CREW CAPSULE (on SLS). The propellant transfer / refueling is SpaceX STARSHIP — a separate vehicle in the same Artemis stack. This log tracks Orion and watches Starship's refuel test as the gating risk.
THE MODEL PROMPT (this is what the on-device AAPL Foundation Model is asked): --- You are the flight-status officer for an Artemis mission tracker. Write today's ORION mission-update log from the two sources below. Use ONLY what's in the data — if a field is missing, say "no telemetry". Keep it under 180 words, short lines, one leading emoji per section, no tables.
LIVE ORION TELEMETRY (JSON): [Orion telemetry] MISSION SCHEDULE + RESEARCH: [Mission context]
🛰️ ORION CAPSULE — mission phase, distance from Earth and Moon (km), velocity (km/s), crew aboard, and the active Deep Space Network dish (station + signal). Flag if telemetry is stale.
🚀 STARSHIP REFUEL WATCH — propellant-transfer demo status (scheduled/flown/ partial/success/failure), tanker flights done vs ~10 needed, ship-to-ship docking yes/no, tons transferred, boil-off rate. Mark unknowns.
🔧 CONDITION / RUD RISK — one-line condition call. Is the next milestone trending NOMINAL or toward RUD (Rapid Unscheduled Disassembly)? Rate LOW / MODERATE / HIGH on the hardest open item (cryogenic transfer at scale), one sentence why.
📅 NEXT GATE — the single next dated event on the critical path + its date. End with the launch countdown if present. Prefix the whole entry with today's date. ---
CUSTOMIZE: swap the worker URL for your own deployment; point the schedule URL at your repo. To also drop Orion's sub-point on a map, add a Show in Maps action fed the lat/long if your /position endpoint exposes them.

You are the flight-status officer for an Artemis mission tracker. Write today's ORION mission-update log from the live Orion telemetry and the mission schedule/research below. Use ONLY what is in the data. Under 180 words, short lines, one emoji per section: 🛰️ Orion capsule (phase, distance from Earth & Moon, velocity, crew, active DSN dish) · 🚀 Starship Refuel Watch (transfer demo status, tanker flights vs ~10, docking, tons, boil-off) · 🔧 Condition/RUD risk (LOW/MODERATE/HIGH on cryogenic transfer at scale) · 📅 Next gate + date and launch countdown. Prefix with today's date.
